blueprint.name: Confirmation dialog
blueprint.description: A modal that asks the user to confirm or cancel a destructive action before it runs. Composes Dialog, Stack, Text, and Button to present a clear question and two explicit choices.

links.role: Hosts the confirmation in a modal surface
Renders the confirm and cancel actions

code.code:
<Dialog id="confirm-delete" header="Delete project?" onClose={onCancel} width={1}>
  <Box padding={4}>
    <Stack space={4}>
      <Text>This permanently deletes the project and all of its content. This action cannot be undone.</Text>
      <Flex gap={2} justify="flex-end">
        <Button text="Cancel" mode="bleed" onClick={onCancel} />
        <Button text="Delete" tone="critical" onClick={onConfirm} />
      </Flex>
    </Stack>
  </Box>
</Dialog>

useCases.description: Guard a destructive or irreversible action — deleting, archiving, or overwriting — so the user confirms intent before it runs.
Confirm low-stakes, easily reversible actions where an undo affordance would serve the user better.

alternative.rationale: A toast with an undo action keeps reversible flows fast and avoids interrupting the user with a modal.

guidelines.guidance: Label the confirm button with the action it performs (e.g. 'Delete') rather than a generic 'OK'.
guidelines.rationale: An action-specific label tells the user what will happen, reducing accidental confirmations.

guidelines.guidance: Give the destructive confirm button the 'critical' tone and keep the cancel action visually quieter.
guidelines.rationale: Tone and weight signal the consequence and steer the user toward the safe default.

guidelines.guidance: Move focus into the dialog on open and return it to the trigger on close.
guidelines.rationale: Keyboard and screen reader users lose their place when focus is not managed across the modal lifecycle.
